recent.statement: Kerch Strait ship transits will drop to 1/day due to intensified Ukrainian conflict.
recent.evidence: Kerch Strait shows 2 daily transits, not the forecasted drop to 1/day, so the specific threshold was not met.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in Japan
recent.evidence: No signal shows a tropical cyclone making landfall in Japan; NOUL-26 is located near the Philippines/Taiwan (18.7,123.5) and the others are far from Japan.

recent.statement: The US will impose new sanctions on Iran
recent.evidence: No captured signal concerns this forecast, and new US sanctions on Iran would certainly have been captured, so no evidence shows the event occurred.

recent.statement: US military strikes on Iran will escalate
recent.evidence: No signal reports any US military strikes on Iran; the Strait of Hormuz signal only shows a 30% drop in ship transits, which is a condition and not evidence of military escalation.

recent.statement: Kerch Strait ship transits will drop below 1/day due to intensified Ukrainian conflict operations
recent.evidence: Kerch Strait shows 2 daily transits, which is above the forecast threshold of below 1/day.

recent.statement: A significant increase in oil prices will occur due to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz
recent.evidence: Brent Crude sits at 100.31 $/bbl and is down 0.38%, showing no significant oil-price increase despite reduced Hormuz transits.

recent.statement: The Strait of Hormuz will experience a brief but significant disruption to shipping within the next week.
recent.evidence: S4 shows Hormuz transits merely 30% below its 30-day average, a routine fluctuation that does not constitute a brief but significant shipping disruption event.

recent.statement: A magnitude 6.0 earthquake will strike off the coast of Japan
recent.evidence: No captured signal reports a magnitude 6.0 earthquake off the coast of Japan during the window, and such an event would certainly have been recorded.

recent.statement: At least one major oil tanker will be affected by the ongoing conflict in Bab el-Mandeb
recent.evidence: S14 shows Bab el-Mandeb at critical/elevated risk status with zero live ships, but no signal reports any oil tanker actually being affected by conflict.

recent.statement: Oil prices will rise by at least $5 per barrel within the next week due to supply chain disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.
recent.evidence: No signal reports oil prices at all; the reduced Hormuz transits in S11 show a supply condition but do not show any $5/barrel price rise occurred.

recent.statement: The price of oil will increase by at least $5 per barrel within the next week due to supply chain disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.
recent.evidence: No signal reports any oil price movement; S11 shows reduced Hormuz transits (a condition, not a $5/barrel price increase), so the forecast's stated threshold is unmet.

recent.statement: The price of oil will rise by at least $5 per barrel within the next week due to supply chain disruptions caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
recent.evidence: No signal reports any oil price movement; S14 only shows an elevated-risk chokepoint status, not a $5/barrel price rise, so the forecast's stated threshold is not met.

self_calibration.note: Published headline probability is RAW conviction (unchanged); this is the honest companion. Calibration fixes overconfidence, not discrimination — see /calibration.
